As Russell was getting into, you get attractive character, right? This is attractive character enhancing. You're going to let people see who you are. They're going to see your face. They're going to see your sincerity in wanting to get answers to this question. And you just get this raw, real, live experience and you get a lot of good response from it, right? You get to connect on a more personal level with them. It has the potential to go viral. Sometimes we see videos where you show the right emotion or you hit on the right, just the right topic and people start sharing it out and then Facebook when they share, then you get free organic traffic and it can go viral or what we call forced viral, which means it has a good enough share rate that if you put some money behind it, it kind of just blows up, right? So if you're not going to be able to do that, you can force it to go viral. But if it's not doing that, putting money behind it, just like putting money in your head against the wall. So if it doesn't have a viral nature, then you can't really force it to go viral. And the fact that they're easy to create on the fly, like that's what probably most of you will do tonight is just hold your phone and do a live video. So this, this right here probably is the place that most of you get a little bit nervous and Facebook is when you start creating your campaigns, right? There's campaigns, there's ad sets and there's ads. Facebook has done a nice job of breaking it out here. What is a campaign about? It's about the objective. The objective you guys want is comments, right? It's about engagement. So don't run it to get clicks to your website or to get anything else or even video views. Run it to get engagement because that's what you want. Okay. For this specific post. And so that's what you would say your lifetime budget is. Try to spend $50 before tomorrow morning-ish, right? Whenever you need this. So yeah, if you have any questions on that, ask me. And then you type out your text. Okay. Any questions on this before I start the video? The video probably answer. I'll take questions after. Okay. So this is how I would create the campaign. I would choose engagement because that's what you want. You want comments, right? In the ad set on the audience side right here on this one, I'm going to restrict my age to people who are likely to want potato gun advice. I'm going to assume that most of them are male, even though there may be some females. What I'm looking for is how can I not, how can I make sure that no of my ad spend dollars, not a man spend dollars go to the wrong place, right? So even though we might lose some women who might be interested in it, I want to be as targeted as possible. That's kind of the mindset when you do choosing target audiences. Okay. I would do at least a double layer of interest targeting. So there is nothing since we are so niched in potato gun, there's, there's no such thing as potato gun interest that you can target in Facebook. So then you have to start thinking outside of the box where else are my people congregating, right? So I did a layer of fireworks and pyrotechnics and smoke bombs and Roman candles and stuff like that. Assuming if you have a 18 year old or 35 year old male in the United States who's interested in fireworks and firecrackers and Roman candles, they might also be interested in potato guns. I did a second layer just to make sure, and I would suggest that most of you do this, a second layer of interest for outdoor enthusiasts, hunters, you know, hiking, fishing, hunting, camping, outdoor recreation. So they need to be in both of these pools. They need to be in this pool somewhere and in this pool somewhere in order for them to see my ad. So I feel like I am fairly targeted with this placements. I would not place on Instagram for this specific ad because you're looking for Facebook comments and you want them to kind of congregate together. So I just restricted it to Facebook. Budget, I mean, this is an engagement ad for 50, 50 to $75. I really hope you'd get enough comments to flesh things out. So that's what I did as a lifetime budget at $50. So what a lead ad is, is in your Facebook news feed, you've got just what looks like a normal ad, but when you click on it, instead of it taking you somewhere, it's actually going to just open up a form right there inside of Facebook. Just opens up this little form box and you fill it out. And now suddenly that person's your lead, right? So you can use it for all kinds of lead generation, but for today you can use it for collecting your 100 answers from 100 people. Any questions on that idea? Yeah. It's really cool. He's nervous, excited. I really want you guys to do this. Like, I don't know how many times I teach people stuff and like, I'm going to do it and they go home and they never do it. This is funny. This is funny. Yesterday, Brandon and Katelyn were in my office and I have a picture of Katelyn in my book where we'll talk about that section day three, like the perfect webinar hack and I was like, there's a picture of me doing it. And I was like, remember you guys did like a month later and she's like, we didn't do a month later. Like, when did you do it? She's like, while you were doing yours, we were planning ours and when yours ended, we launched ours right afterwards. Like that's the difference between them and like every, like most people I know in the world, like they're implementing the second it's happening. So I don't want you guys like, oh, cool. I'll do Facebook live on this. And then next week you're like, what was that thing again? Now while you're in the moment, while you remember why it's important, why you saw me do it. So you don't forget and we can get it. And in worst case, like this will be the worst one you ever do. The next one you do will be better. Okay. Every time you do it, it gets better and better, but we just needed everyone to do it just to do it. So any questions about that? Okay.
